member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.TestRun.TestJobData.GetCurrentBuildOptions
member.summary: Fetches configured build options, both PlayerBuildOptionsWithProfile and PlayerBuildOptions are initialized by .

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings
member.summary: A set of execution settings defining how to run tests, using the .

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.#ctor(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter[])
member.summary: Creates an instance with a given set of filters, if any.
member.param: Set of filters

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.overloadTestRunSettings
member.summary: An instance of to set up before running tests on a Player.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.filters
member.summary: A collection of to execute tests on.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.runSynchronously
member.summary: Note that this is only supported for EditMode tests, and that tests which take multiple frames (i.e. [UnityTest] tests, or tests with [UnitySetUp] or [UnityTearDown] scaffolding) will be filtered out.
member.value: If true, the call to Execute() will run tests synchronously, guaranteeing that all tests have finished running by the time the call returns.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.playerHeartbeatTimeout
member.summary: The time, in seconds, the editor should wait for heartbeats after starting a test run on a player. This defaults to 10 minutes.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.targetPlatform
member.summary: The platform to run the test on. If set to null, then the Editor is the target for the tests.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings.ToString
member.summary: Implementation of ToString() that builds a string composed of the execution settings.
member.returns: The current execution settings as a string.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.IErrorCallbacks
member.summary: An extended version of the , which get invoked if the test run fails due to a build error or if any has a failure.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.IErrorCallbacks.OnError(System.String)
member.summary: Method invoked on failure.
member.param: The error message detailing the reason for the run to fail.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestRunSettings
member.summary: ITestRunSettings lets you set any of the global settings right before building a Player for a test run and then reverts the settings afterward. ITestRunSettings implements [IDisposable](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.idisposable?view=netframework-4.8), and runs after building the Player with tests.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestRunSettings.Apply
member.summary: A method called before building the Player.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.RunState
member.summary: The RunState enum indicates whether a test can be executed.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.RunState.NotRunnable
member.summary: The test is not runnable.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.RunState.Runnable
member.summary: The test is runnable.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.RunState.Explicit
member.summary: The test can only be run explicitly

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.RunState.Skipped
member.summary: The test has been skipped. This value may appear on a Test when certain attributes are used to skip the test.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.RunState.Ignored
member.summary: The test has been ignored. May appear on a Test, when the IgnoreAttribute is used.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestMode
member.summary: A flag indicating whether to run Edit Mode or Play Mode tests.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestMode.EditMode
member.summary: Run EditMode tests.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestMode.PlayMode
member.summary: Run PlayMode tests.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor
member.summary: The `ITestResultAdaptor` is the representation of the test results for a node in the test tree implemented as a wrapper around the [NUnit](http://www.nunit.org/) [ITest](https://github.com/nunit/nunit/blob/master/src/NUnitFramework/framework/Interfaces/ITestResults.cs) interface.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.Test
member.summary: The test details of the test result tree node as a

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.Name
member.summary: The name of the test node.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.FullName
member.summary: Gets the full name of the test result
member.value: The name of the test result.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.ResultState
member.summary: Gets the state of the result as a string.
member.value: It returns one of these values: `Inconclusive`, `Skipped`, `Skipped:Ignored`, `Skipped:Explicit`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Failed:Error`, `Failed:Cancelled`, `Failed:Invalid`

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.TestStatus
member.summary: Gets the status of the test as an enum.
member.value: It returns one of these values:`Inconclusive`, `Skipped`, `Passed`, or `Failed`

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.Duration
member.summary: Gets the elapsed time for running the test in seconds

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.StartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the time the test started running.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.EndTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the time the test finished running.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.Message
member.summary: The message associated with a test failure or with not running the test

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.StackTrace
member.summary: Any stacktrace associated with an error or failure. Not available in the Compact Framework 1.0.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.AssertCount
member.summary: The number of asserts executed when running the test and all its children.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.FailCount
member.summary: The number of test cases that failed when running the test and all its children.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.PassCount
member.summary: The number of test cases that passed when running the test and all its children.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.SkipCount
member.summary: The number of test cases that were skipped when running the test and all its children.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.InconclusiveCount
member.summary: The number of test cases that were inconclusive when running the test and all its children.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.HasChildren
member.summary: Accessing HasChildren should not force creation of the Children collection in classes implementing this interface.
member.value: True if this result has any child results.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.Children
member.summary: Gets the the collection of child results.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.Output
member.summary: Gets any text output written to this result.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor.ToXml
member.summary: Use this to save the results to an XML file
member.returns: The test results as an `NUnit` XML node.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor
member.summary: ```ITestAdaptor``` is a representation of a node in the test tree implemented as a wrapper around the [NUnit](http://www.nunit.org/) [ITest](https://github.com/nunit/nunit/blob/master/src/NUnitFramework/framework/Interfaces/ITest.cs) interface.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Id
member.summary: The ID of the test tree node. The ID can change if you add new tests to the suite. Use UniqueName, if you want to have a more permanent point of reference.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Name
member.summary: The name of the test. E.g.,```MyTest```.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.FullName
member.summary: The full name of the test. E.g., ```MyNamespace.MyTestClass.MyTest```.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.TestCaseCount
member.summary: The total number of test cases in the node and all sub-nodes.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.HasChildren
member.summary: Whether the node has any children.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.IsSuite
member.summary: True if the node is a test suite/fixture, false otherwise.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Children
member.summary: The child nodes.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Parent
member.summary: The parent node, if any.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.TestCaseTimeout
member.summary: The test case timeout in milliseconds. Note that this value is only available on TestFinished.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.TypeInfo
member.summary: The type of test class as an ```NUnit``` . If the node is not a test class, then the value is null.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Method
member.summary: The Nunit of the test method. If the node is not a test method, then the value is null.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Arguments
member.summary: The array of arguments that the test method/fixture will be invoked with.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Categories
member.summary: An array of the categories applied to the test or fixture.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.IsTestAssembly
member.summary: Returns true if the node represents a test assembly, false otherwise.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.RunState
member.summary: The run state of the test node. Either ```NotRunnable```, ```Runnable```, ```Explicit```, ```Skipped```, or ```Ignored```.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.Description
member.summary: The description of the test.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.SkipReason
member.summary: The skip reason. E.g., if ignoring the test.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.ParentId
member.summary: The ID of the parent node.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.ParentFullName
member.summary: The full name of the parent node.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.UniqueName
member.summary: A unique generated name for the test node. E.g., ```Tests.dll/MyNamespace/MyTestClass/[Tests][MyNamespace.MyTestClass.MyTest]```.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.ParentUniqueName
member.summary: A unique name of the parent node. E.g., ```Tests.dll/MyNamespace/[Tests][MyNamespace.MyTestClass][suite]```.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.ChildIndex
member.summary: The child index of the node in its parent.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor.TestMode
member.summary: The mode of the test. Either **Edit Mode** or **Play Mode**.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter
member.summary: The filter class provides the with a specification of what tests to run when [running tests programmatically](https://docs.unity3d.com/Packages/com.unity.test-framework@1.1/manual/extension-run-tests.html).

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.testMode
member.summary: An enum flag that specifies if Edit Mode or Play Mode tests should run.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.testNames
member.summary: The full name of the tests to match the filter. This is usually in the format FixtureName.TestName. If the test has test arguments, then include them in parenthesis. E.g. MyTestClass2.MyTestWithMultipleValues(1).

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.groupNames
member.summary: The same as testNames, except that it allows for Regex. This is useful for running specific fixtures or namespaces. E.g. "^MyNamespace\\." Runs any tests where the top namespace is MyNamespace.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.categoryNames
member.summary: The name of a [Category](https://nunit.org/docs/2.2.7/category.html) to include in the run. Any test or fixtures runs that have a Category matching the string.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.assemblyNames
member.summary: The name of assemblies included in the run. That is the assembly name, without the .dll file extension. E.g., MyTestAssembly

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.targetPlatform
member.summary: The platform to run the test on. If set to null, then the Editor is the target for the tests. Obsolete. Use the targetPlatform property on the .

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.Filter.ToString
member.summary: Implementation of ToString() that builds a string composed of the filter values.
member.returns: The current filter values as a string.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestStatus
member.summary: The TestStatus enum indicates the test result status.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestStatus.Inconclusive
member.summary: The test ran with an inconclusive result.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestStatus.Skipped
member.summary: The test was skipped.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestStatus.Passed
member.summary: The test ran and passed.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestStatus.Failed
member.summary: The test ran and failed.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ICallbacks
member.summary: Callbacks in the for the test stages when running tests.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ICallbacks.RunStarted(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor)
member.summary: A callback invoked when a test run is started.
member.param: The full loaded test tree.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ICallbacks.RunFinished(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor)
member.summary: A callback invoked when a test run is finished.
member.param: The result of the test run.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ICallbacks.TestStarted(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor)
member.summary: A callback invoked when each individual node of the test tree has started executing.
member.param: The test node currently executed.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ICallbacks.TestFinished(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor)
member.summary: A callback invoked when each individual node of the test tree has finished executing.
member.param: The result of the test tree node after it had been executed.

summary.para: The TestRunnerApi retrieves and runs tests programmatically from code inside the project, or inside other packages. TestRunnerApi is a [ScriptableObject](https://docs.unity3d.com/ScriptReference/ScriptableObject.html). You can initialize the API like this:

Note: You can subscribe and receive test results in one instance of the API, even if the run starts from another instance. The TestRunnerApi supports the following workflows: - [How to run tests programmatically](https://docs.unity3d.com/Packages/com.unity.test-framework@1.1/manual/extension-run-tests.html) - [How to get test results](https://docs.unity3d.com/Packages/com.unity.test-framework@1.1/manual/extension-get-test-results.html) - [How to retrieve the list of tests](https://docs.unity3d.com/Packages/com.unity.test-framework@1.1/manual/extension-retrieve-test-list.html)

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.Execute(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ExecutionSettings)
member.summary: Starts a test run with a given set of executionSettings.
member.param: Set of
member.returns: A GUID that identifies the TestJobData.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.RegisterCallbacks``1(``0,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets up a given instance of to be invoked on test runs.
member.typeparam: Generic representing a type of callback.
member.param: The test callbacks to be invoked.
Sets the order in which the callbacks are invoked, starting with the highest value first.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.RegisterTestCallback``1(``0,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets up a given instance of to be invoked on test runs.
member.typeparam: Generic representing a type of callback.
member.param: The test callbacks to be invoked
Sets the order in which the callbacks are invoked, starting with the highest value first.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.UnregisterCallbacks``1(``0)
member.summary: Unregister an instance of to no longer receive callbacks from test runs.
member.typeparam: Generic representing a type of callback.
member.param: The test callbacks to unregister.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.UnregisterTestCallback``1(``0)
member.summary: Unregister an instance of to no longer receive callbacks from test runs.
member.typeparam: Generic representing a type of callback.
member.param: The test callbacks to unregister.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.RetrieveTestList(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestMode,System.Action{UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestAdaptor})
member.summary: Retrieve the full test tree as ITestAdaptor for a given test mode. This is obsolete. Use TestRunnerApi.RetrieveTestTree instead.
member.param: The TestMode to retrieve the test list for.
A callback that is invoked when the test tree is retrieved.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.SaveResultToFile(UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.ITestResultAdaptor,System.String)
member.summary: Save a given set of ITestResultAdaptor in [NUnit XML Format](https://docs.nunit.org/articles/nunit/technical-notes/usage/Test-Result-XML-Format.html) to a file at the provided file path. Any matching existing file is overwritten.
member.param: Test results to write in a file.
An xml file path relative to the project folder.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.Api.TestRunnerApi.CancelTestRun(System.String)
member.summary: Cancel the test run with a given guid string. The guid string can be retrieved when executing the test run. The test run may take multiple frames to finish cleaning up from the test run. Any current active test will be marked as "Canceled" and any other remaining tests marked as "NotRun".
member.param: Test run guid string.
member.returns: A boolean indicating whether canceling of the given job was successful. Canceling of a job will not be a success if no test job is found matching the guid, if the job is not currently or the job is already canceling.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.CommandLineTest.TestRunState.NoCallbacksReceived
member.summary: When the test run has started, but no callbacks from the test runner api have yet been received.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.CommandLineTest.TestRunState.OneOrMoreTestsExecutedWithNoFailures
member.summary: When at least one test started event have been fired for a test node.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.CommandLineTest.TestRunState.OneOrMoreTestsExecutedWithOneOrMoreFailed
member.summary: When at least one test finished event have been fired for a test node and the status is failed.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.CommandLineTest.TestRunState.CompletedJobWithoutAnyTestsExecuted
member.summary: When the test job in the test runner api have completed, but no test started events for test nodes has happened. E.g. if there are no valid tests in the project.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.CommandLineTest.TestRunState.RunError
member.summary: When the test runner api has raised an error during the run.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ITestScriptAssetsCreator
member.summary: Provides an interface for creating test assets from templates.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ITestScriptAssetsCreator.AddNewFolderWithTestAssemblyDefinition(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new folder in the active folder path with an associated Test Script Assembly definition.
member.param: Should the assembly definition be editor-only?

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ITestScriptAssetsCreator.ActiveFolderContainsTestAssemblyDefinition
member.summary: Checks if the active folder path already contains a Test Script Assembly definition.
member.returns: True if the active folder path contains a Test Script Assembly; false otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ITestScriptAssetsCreator.AddNewTestScript
member.summary: Adds a new Test Script asset in the active folder path.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ITestScriptAssetsCreator.TestScriptWillCompileInActiveFolder
member.summary: Checks if a Test Script asset can be compiled in the active folder path.
member.returns: True if a Test Script can be compiled in the active folder path; false otherwise.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IActiveFolderTemplateAssetCreator
member.summary: Provides basic utility methods for creating assets in the active Project Browser folder path.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IActiveFolderTemplateAssetCreator.GetActiveFolderPath
member.summary: The active Project Browser folder path relative to the root project folder.
member.returns: The active folder path string.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IActiveFolderTemplateAssetCreator.CreateFolderWithTemplates(System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Creates a new folder asset in the active folder path with assets defined by provided templates.
member.param: The default name of the folder.
The names of templates to be used when creating embedded assets.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IActiveFolderTemplateAssetCreator.CreateScriptAssetFromTemplateFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new script asset in the active folder path defined by the provided template.
member.param: The default name of the new script asset.
The template to be used when creating the asset.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ICustomScriptAssemblyMappingFinder
member.summary: Provides mapping information from folder paths to their corresponding Custom Script Assembly scope.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ICustomScriptAssemblyMappingFinder.FindCustomScriptAssemblyFromFolderPath(System.String)
member.summary: Finds the Custom Script Assembly associated with the provided folder path.
member.param: The folder path to check.
member.returns: The associated ; null if none.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.FolderPathTestCompilationContextProvider.FolderPathBelongsToCustomTestAssembly(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the provided folder path belongs to a Custom Test Assembly. A Custom Test Assembly is defined by a valid reference to the precompiled NUnit library.
member.param: The folder path to check.
member.returns: True if a custom test assembly associated with the provided folder can be found; false otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.FolderPathTestCompilationContextProvider.TestScriptWillCompileInFolderPath(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the provided folder path belongs to an assembly capable of compiling Test Scripts. Unless the setting is enabled, a Test Script can only be compiled in a Custom Test Assembly or an (implicit or explicit) assembly.
member.param: The folder path to check.
member.returns: True if Test Scripts can be successfully compiled when added to this folder path; false otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.FolderPathTestCompilationContextProvider.FolderPathBelongsToImplicitEditorAssembly(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the provided folder path is a special editor path that belongs to an implicit editor assembly.
member.param: The folder path to check.
member.returns: True if the folder path belongs to an implicit editor assembly; false otherwise.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IFolderPathTestCompilationContextProvider
member.summary: Provides Test Script compilation context associated with project folder paths.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IFolderPathTestCompilationContextProvider.FolderPathBelongsToCustomTestAssembly(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the provided folder path belongs to a Custom Test Assembly.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.IFolderPathTestCompilationContextProvider.TestScriptWillCompileInFolderPath(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the provided folder path belongs to an assembly capable of compiling Test Scripts.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.CustomScriptAssemblyMappingFinder.FindCustomScriptAssemblyFromFolderPath(System.String)
member.exception: The provided string argument is null.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.CustomScriptAssemblyMappingFinder.FindCustomScriptAssemblyFromScriptPath(System.String)
member.summary: Finds the Custom Script Assembly associated with the provided script asset path.
member.param: The script path to check.
member.returns: The associated ; null if none.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.CustomScriptAssemblyMappingFinder.CustomScriptAssemblyWrapper
member.summary: Custom Script Assembly wrapper.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.CustomScriptAssemblyMappingFinder.CustomScriptAssemblyWrapper.#ctor(UnityEditor.Scripting.ScriptCompilation.CustomScriptAssembly)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the class.
member.param: The to be represented by the wrapper.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.TestScriptAssetMenuItems
member.summary: The set of Menu Items dedicated to creating test assets: Test Scripts and Custom Test Assemblies.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.TestScriptAssetMenuItems.AddNewFolderWithTestAssemblyDefinition
member.summary: Adds a new folder asset and an associated Custom Test Assembly in the active folder path.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.TestScriptAssetMenuItems.CanAddNewFolderWithTestAssemblyDefinition
member.summary: Checks if it is possible to add a new Custom Test Assembly inside the active folder path.
member.returns: False if the active folder path already contains a Custom Test Assembly; true otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.TestScriptAssetMenuItems.AddNewTestScript
member.summary: Adds a new Test Script asset in the active folder path.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.TestScriptAssetMenuItems.CanAddNewTestScript
member.summary: Checks if it is possible to add a new Test Script in the active folder path.
member.returns: True if a Test Script can be compiled in the active folder path; false otherwise.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ICustomScriptAssembly
member.summary: Provides a wrapper for a Custom Script Assembly and exposes its basic properties.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ICustomScriptAssembly.HasPrecompiledReference(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the Custom Script Assembly is referencing the provided precompiled library.
member.param: The name of the precompiled library reference to be checked.
member.returns: True if the assembly references the provided precompiled library; false otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.TestAssets.ICustomScriptAssembly.HasAssemblyFlag(UnityEditor.Scripting.ScriptCompilation.AssemblyFlags)
member.summary: Checks if the Custom Script Assembly has the provided value set.
member.param: The value to check against.
member.returns: True if the provided value is set; false otherwise.

member.summary: A flag enum content provider to be used with the control.

member.summary: Creates a new instance of the class.
member.param: The initial selection value.
The callback to be invoked on selection change.
member.exception: Thrown if the generic enum parameter type is not integer based or if the initial selection value is empty.
Thrown if the provided change callback is null.

member.summary: A generic type content provider to be used with the control.
member.typeparam: The type of values represented by content elements.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.GenericItemContentProvider`1.#ctor(`0,UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectableItem{`0}[],System.Int32[],System.Action{`0})
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the class.
member.param: The initial selection value.
The set of selectable items.
The indices of items which should be followed by separator lines.
member.exception: Thrown if any of the provided arguments is null, except for the separator indices.
Thrown if the set of items is empty or does not contain the initial selection value.

member.summary: A default implementation of the interface.
member.typeparam: The type of the value represented by this content element.

member.summary: Creates a new instance of the class

The display name of this item.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider
member.summary: Defines a content provider that can be used with the control.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider.Count
member.summary: The total number of items to display.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider.IsMultiSelection
member.summary: Multiple selection support. Multiple selection dropdowns don't get closed on selection change.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider.SeparatorIndices
member.summary: The indices of items which should be followed by separator lines.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider.GetName(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the display name of the item at the specified index.
member.param: The index of the item whose display name is to be returned.
member.returns: The display name of the item at the specified index.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider.SelectItem(System.Int32)
member.summary: Signals a request to select the item at the specified index.
member.param: The index of the item to be selected.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.ISelectionDropDownContentProvider.IsSelected(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the selection status of the item at the specified index.
member.param: The index of the item whose selection status is to be returned.
member.returns: true if the item is currently selected; otherwise, false.

member.summary: Provides methods for dealing with common bit operations.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestRunner.GUI.Controls.BitUtility.GetCardinality(System.Int32)
member.summary: Evaluates the cardinality of an integer, treating the value as a bit set. Optimization based on http://graphics.stanford.edu/~seander/bithacks.html#CountBitsSetParallel.
member.param: The input integer value.
member.returns: The number of bits set in the provided input integer value.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.RequirePlatformSupportAttribute
member.summary: The `RequirePlatformSupportAttribute` attribute can be applied to test assemblies (will affect every test in the assembly), fixtures (will affect every test in the fixture), or to individual test methods.

member.summary: The build target platform, see [BuildTarget](https://docs.unity3d.com/ScriptReference/BuildTarget.html).

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.ITestPlayerBuildModifier
member.summary: An interface for a callback modifying the when building a player for running tests in the runtime.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.ITestPlayerBuildModifier.ModifyOptions(UnityEditor.BuildPlayerOptions)
member.summary: A callback to modify the when building a player for test run. Return the modified version of the provided build options.
member.param: The unmodified BuildPlayerOptions.
member.returns: The modified BuildPlayerOptions.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.AssetPipelineIgnore
member.summary: Ignore attributes dedicated to Asset Import Pipeline backend version handling.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.AssetPipelineIgnore.IgnoreInV1
member.summary: Ignore the test when running with the legacy Asset Import Pipeline V1 backend.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestTools.AssetPipelineIgnore.IgnoreInV2
member.summary: Ignore the test when running with the latest Asset Import Pipeline V2 backend.

summary.para:
You can use the ```TestPlayerBuildModifier``` attribute to accomplish a couple of different scenarios. ## Modify the Player build options for Play Mode tests
It is possible to change the [BuildPlayerOptions](https://docs.unity3d.com/ScriptReference/BuildPlayerOptions.html) for the test **Player**, to achieve custom behavior when running **Play Mode** tests. Modifying the build options allows for changing the target location of the build as well as changing [BuildOptions](https://docs.unity3d.com/ScriptReference/BuildOptions.html).
To modify the `BuildPlayerOptions`, do the following:
* Implement the `ITestPlayerBuildModifier` * Reference the implementation type in a `TestPlayerBuildModifier` attribute on an assembly level.

> **Note:** When building the Player, it includes all `TestPlayerBuildModifier` attributes across all loaded assemblies, independent of the currently used test filter. As the implementation references the `UnityEditor` namespace, the code is typically implemented in an Editor only assembly, as the `UnityEditor` namespace is not available otherwise.
## Split build and run It is possible to use the Unity Editor for building the Player with tests, without [running the tests](./workflow-run-playmode-test-standalone.md). This allows for running the Player on e.g. another machine. In this case, it is necessary to modify the Player to build and implement a custom handling of the test result. By using `TestPlayerBuildModifier`, you can alter the `BuildOptions` to not start the Player after the build as well as build the Player at a specific location. Combined with [PostBuildCleanup](./reference-setup-and-cleanup.md#prebuildsetup-and-postbuildcleanup), you can automatically exit the Editor on completion of the build.

example.para:
If the Editor is still running after the Play Mode tests have run, the Player tries to report the results back, using [PlayerConnection](https://docs.unity3d.com/ScriptReference/Networking.PlayerConnection.PlayerConnection.html), which has a reference to the IP address of the Editor machine, when built.
To implement a custom way of reporting the results of the test run, let one of the assemblies in the Player include a `TestRunCallbackAttribute`. At `RunFinished`, it is possible to get the full test report as XML from the [NUnit](http://www.nunit.org/) test result by calling `result.ToXml(true)`. You can save the result and then save it on the device or send it to another machine as needed.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.TestTools.TestPlayerBuildModifierAttribute.#ctor(System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes and returns an instance of TestPlayerBuildModifierAttribute or throws an .
member.param: A target type that implements ITestPlayerBuildModifier.
member.exception: Throws a if the type provided does not implemented the `ITestPlayerBuildModifier` interface.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.ITestRunDataHolder
member.summary: No longer in use.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.ITestRunDataHolder.TestRunDataList
member.summary: Gets the list of test run data.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.TestRunDataHolder
member.summary: No longer in use.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.TestRunData
member.summary: Represents the data for a test run.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.TestRunData.SuiteName
member.summary: The name of the test suite.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.TestRunData.TestsInFixture
member.summary: The names of the tests in the fixture.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.TestRunData.OneTimeSetUpDuration
member.summary: The duration of the one-time setup.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.TestRunner.UnityTestProtocol.TestRunData.OneTimeTearDownDuration
member.summary: The duration of the one-time teardown.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.TestTools.EnterPlayMode
member.summary: Implements . Creates a yield instruction to enter Play Mode.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.EnterPlayMode.ExpectDomainReload
member.summary: Returns true if the instruction expects a domain reload to occur.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.EnterPlayMode.ExpectedPlaymodeState
member.summary: Returns true if the instruction expects the Unity Editor to be in **Play Mode**.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.EnterPlayMode.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: When creating an Editor test that uses the UnityTest attribute, use this to trigger the Editor to enter Play Mode. Throws an exception if the Editor is already in Play Mode or if there is a script compilation error.
member.param: A flag indication whether to expect a domain reload.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.EnterPlayMode.Perform
member.summary: Performs the multi-step instructions of entering PlayMode.
member.returns: An IEnumerator with the async steps.
member.exception: An exception is thrown if the editor is already in PlayMode or if script compilation failed.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.TestTools.WaitForDomainReload
member.summary: WaitForDomainReload is an that you can yield in Edit Mode tests. It delays the execution of scripts until after an incoming domain reload. If the domain reload results in a script compilation failure, then it throws an exception.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.WaitForDomainReload.#ctor
member.summary: Create a new instance of the `WaitForDomainReload` yield instruction.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.WaitForDomainReload.ExpectDomainReload
member.summary: Returns true if the instruction expects a domain reload to occur.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.WaitForDomainReload.ExpectedPlaymodeState
member.summary: Returns true if the instruction expects the Unity Editor to be in **Play Mode**.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.WaitForDomainReload.Perform
member.summary: Perform the multi step action of waiting for a domain reload.
member.returns: An IEnumerator with steps.
member.exception: Throws an exception if script compilation failed or if the expected domain reload did not occur.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.TestTools.ExitPlayMode
member.summary: Implements . A new instance of the class is a yield instruction to exit Play Mode.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.ExitPlayMode.ExpectDomainReload
member.summary: Gets the value of ExpectDomainReload

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.ExitPlayMode.ExpectedPlaymodeState
member.summary: Gets the value of ExpectedPlaymodeState

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.ExitPlayMode.#ctor
member.summary: Sets ExpectDomainReload and ExpectedPlaymodeState to false.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.ExitPlayMode.Perform
member.summary: Performs the multi-step instruction of exiting PlayMode.
member.returns: An IEnumerator with the async steps.
member.exception: An exception is thrown if the editor is not in PlayMode.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts
member.summary: `RecompileScripts` is an that you can yield in Edit Mode tests. It lets you trigger a recompilation of scripts in the Unity Editor.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the `RecompileScripts` yield instruction.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the `RecompileScripts` yield instruction.
member.param: This parameter indicates if you expect a script compilation to start (defaults to true). If a script compilation does not start and `expectScriptCompilation` is true, then it throws an exception.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the `RecompileScripts` yield instruction.
member.param: This parameter indicates if you expect a script compilation to start (defaults to true). If a script compilation does not start and `expectScriptCompilation` is `true`, then it throws an exception.
This parameter indicates if you expect a script compilation to succeed. If not succeeded then an exception will be thrown.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.ExpectDomainReload
member.summary: Returns true if the instruction expects a domain reload to occur.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.ExpectedPlaymodeState
member.summary: Returns true if the instruction expects the Unity Editor to be in **Play Mode**.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.ExpectScriptCompilation
member.summary: Indicates whether a script compilation is expected.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.ExpectScriptCompilationSuccess
member.summary: Indicates whether the expected script compilation is expected to succeed.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.Current
member.summary: The current active instance of the RecompileScripts yield instruction.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.TestTools.RecompileScripts.Perform
member.summary: Perform the multi step instruction of triggering a recompilation of scripts and waiting for its completion.
member.returns: An IEnumerator with the async steps.
member.exception: Throws an exception if the editor does not need to recompile scripts or if the script compilation failed when expected to succeed.
